title: Incremental orders mart
prompt: Build an incremental model `orders_mart` from `stg_orders`. Materialize it as incremental with unique_key='order_id', and use is_incremental() so a run only processes rows newer than what's already loaded (updated_at greater than the max already in the model). It must end up matching a full rebuild after new and restated orders arrive.
sources: CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE raw_orders (order_id INTEGER, customer_id INTEGER, amount DECIMAL(10,2), updated_at TIMESTAMP);

checks.message: orders_mart must be an incremental model (config materialized='incremental') that reads from {{ ref('stg_orders') }} and guards new rows with is_incremental() — a plain table isn't enough.

models/orders_mart.sql: -- Build the incremental orders_mart from ref('stg_orders').

hints: Guard the filter with {% if is_incremental() %} … {% endif %} and compare against {{ this }}.
